mnemonics: DISC H.A.T. - Disk Scheduling Head, Access Time (seek time + rotational latency + transfer time), and Tracks
keyPoints: Disk scheduling algorithms (FCFS, SSTF, SCAN, C-SCAN, LOOK, C-LOOK) aim to minimize seek time.
RAID levels (0, 1, 4, 5, 6) combine multiple disks for performance, redundancy, or both.
The operating system uses a swap space or page file on mass storage to extend virtual memory.
commonMistakes: Confusing RAID 1 (mirroring) with RAID 5 (striping with distributed parity) and their respective trade-offs between performance, capacity, and redundancy.
Assuming that a shorter seek time always means better overall performance, neglecting the impact of rotational latency and transfer time.
Believing the C-SCAN (Circular SCAN) algorithm services requests in both directions; it only services requests in one direction and returns to the start.
